rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target component or implementation scope, preserving the AADL architecture
Place component type contracts in the component type annex, and implementation-level bindings in the implementation annex
Use visible identifiers from the current scope, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for const and eq declarations
generic_rules: Preserve the existing AADL architecture when inserting AGREE annex blocks
Use visible identifiers from the current scope, and avoid implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Use AGREE scalar types (int, real, bool) for const and eq declarations
Align helper declaration types with the expression types in comparisons
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use initialized temporal recurrence for stateful equations, e.g., `0.0 -> pre(t) + step`
Use nested `if ... then ... else` for branching logic
Use AGREE logical operators such as `and`, `or`, `not(...)`, and implication `=>`
anti_patterns: Do not use implementation-only subcomponent paths in component type contracts
Do not use opaque Boolean data range comparisons unless the requirement and AADL data representation provide a numeric scalar
Do not convert Boolean domain values into integers
irrelevant_topics: AGREE, annex, assume, guarantee, assign, validator, or repair
